broadcast.text: You know who else got called crazy? Every genius ever. Five backs is five weekly matchup problems for OPPONENTS. Pittman at one-oh-one just makes it unfair.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Aaron Jones makes FIVE running backs. Five. The booth has stopped recommending receivers and started documenting evidence. Somewhere a bye-week calculator is quietly weeping.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: FOUR running backs means NEVER punting. And Godwin at ninety-two? Veteran hands falling into the perfect situation. The plan is coming together and the haters know it.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Tony Pollard. A fourth running back. The booth's receiver recommendations are now zero for three, not through error but through stubbornness. DK Metcalf went eleven picks ago to someone who wanted him.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Sniped by a CPU?! That's not a miss, that's SABOTAGE. And DK Metcalf falling to seventy-seven is the universe apologizing. Take the gift.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Brian Thomas Junior goes ONE PICK before D1LLIGAF's turn. The booth said sixty-eight was the window. The booth was ignored. The window closed. We're not saying it - the board is.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: The RB room is now a FORTRESS. Taylor, Henderson, Montgomery - you don't need receivers when you never face third and long. Visionary.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: David Montgomery over the booth's on-record receiver call. D1LLIGAF hears advice the way he hears lane-assist warnings - as a suggestion. Sixty-eight will be relitigated in December.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: One receiver in and the offense is ALREADY complete. Whatever happens at sixty-eight is a bonus round. That's how far ahead this roster is.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Egbuka finally gives D1LLIGAF a receiver, and the sixty-eight pick is where rosters quietly get won. The booth wants Brian Thomas Junior here and will say so on the record.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Taylor, Allen, Bowers, Henderson - the man drafted an entire OFFENSE first. Receivers are just accessories when the engine is this pretty.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Through four rounds D1LLIGAF has built a gorgeous roster with one glaring omission: anyone who catches passes. Fifty-three is where that gets addressed, or the booth starts asking pointed questions.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: The man is being DELIBERATE. Taylor, Allen, and now the whole TE aisle to himself. You think that's an accident? That's chess.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: A five-pick run of wideouts and workhorses later, D1LLIGAF sits at twenty-nine staring at a board with no receivers on it. The booth notes the pause clock got a workout there.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: OBJECTIVELY A SPINE?! It's the best start in recorded draft history. Taylor. Allen. The dynasty is assembling itself in real time, people.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Josh Allen to D1LLIGAF at twenty and the booth must concede: Taylor plus Allen through two rounds is objectively a spine. The rest of the lobby responds by drafting quarterbacks nobody asked about.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: Jonathan Taylor at five was surgical. And now the greatest QB class in mock history falls right into his lap at twenty? The lobby is CRYING.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Fourteen picks flew by like a commercial break and the running back shelf got stripped to the studs. Now D1LLIGAF stares at a wall of quarterbacks at twenty. Value or trap? We're about to find out.
broadcast.speaker: anchor

broadcast.text: A fresh draft and destiny STILL hands the franchise pick five. You can't script this - well, you can, and the script says greatness.
broadcast.speaker: homer

broadcast.text: Take two: same lobby, same slot, and the same four names off the board before D1LLIGAF touches his keyboard. Five is where drafts are won or wasted, folks.
broadcast.speaker: anchor
